mentions.note: Recently downgraded; faces restaurant sector headwinds; can't pound table on food inflation.
Down from 169 to attractive 23x earnings; asset-light model with excellent growth; value meal offering resilient to global economic stress regardless of oil/war impacts.
Down to $153 (from $169) at 23x earnings; excellent growth with asset-light model; nice value meal business resilient to oil prices and economic weakness; good value play.
Amazing quarter reflecting Brian Niccol disciples' focus on speed of service, customer experience, loyalty program. Jumped 3% but underperforming Brinker; still executing excellently.

Pure-play KFC and Taco Bell (two amazing performers) post-Pizza Hut divestiture. Currently trading well below justified levels despite removal of drag; near-term catalyst: longrange capital acquisition close.

Pizza Hut divestiture ($1.5B to Long Range Capital, $1.2B to Yum China) frees KFC and Taco Bell to accelerate growth. Taco Bell 18% same-store growth, 1,200 units outside US with massive whitespace. KFC building one every 3 hours globally with Korea momentum and UK success. $4B buyback approved. Digital 60% of business. Following Darden's Red Lobster exit template — nothing but up.
